name: Current Affairs in History (3): India's 2nd Astronaut & the Mughal 500th Anniversary

sourceRefs.label: Wikipedia/oneindia.com - Shubhanshu Shukla's June 2025 spaceflight (Axiom Mission-4); Mughal Empire's 500th anniversary (1526-2026)

researchNotes: Third History current-affairs topic (3rd lap). The Shubhanshu Shukla fact is unusually valuable: it's a genuine historic FIRST/SECOND (India's 2nd-ever astronaut, 41 years after the first), cleanly dated, and naturally bridges History and Science — exactly the kind of durable 'current affairs become permanent history' fact this rotation track is meant to capture. Expansion pass (Batch 14): added Axiom-4 mission specifics (Shukla's pilot role, the 'Gaganyatri' title, mission duration/distance, the seven microgravity experiments, and its role as a Gaganyaan readiness test).

reading.intro: Covers a genuine historic milestone — India's second-ever astronaut — and a major historical commemoration: the Mughal Empire's 500th founding anniversary.

qa.question: Who became the SECOND Indian to travel to space, when, and how?
qa.answer: Wing Commander Shubhanshu Shukla, on 25 June 2025 — flying to the International Space Station (ISS) aboard SpaceX's Axiom Mission-4.

qa.question: Who was the FIRST Indian to travel to space, and when, for context?
qa.answer: Rakesh Sharma, in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 — meaning Shubhanshu Shukla's 2025 flight came 41 years after India's first astronaut.

qa.question: What major historical anniversary falls in 2026, and what does it mark?
qa.answer: The 500th anniversary of the founding of the Mughal Empire — founded on 21 April 1526 (following Babur's victory at the First Battle of Panipat). The empire's architectural legacy includes the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort, and Red Fort.

qa.question: What was 'Sewa Parv 2025', organised by the Ministry of Culture?
qa.answer: A cultural initiative held 17 September - 2 October 2025, featuring art workshops at 75 locations across India.

qa.question: What was Shubhanshu Shukla's specific role on Axiom Mission-4, and what title did ISRO give him?
qa.answer: He served as PILOT of the mission (an Indian Air Force test pilot and ISRO astronaut-designate) — becoming the first Indian to visit the International Space Station. ISRO referred to him as a 'GAGANYATRI'.

qa.question: How long did the Axiom Mission-4 flight last, and how many orbits/how much distance did it cover?
qa.answer: Nearly 20 days, covering approximately 12 million kilometres across 282 orbits of the ISS. It launched on 25 June 2025 and the mission was successfully completed with splashdown/return on 15 July 2025.

qa.question: How many microgravity experiments did Shukla conduct on Axiom-4, and what did they study?
qa.answer: Seven microgravity experiments, developed by ISRO and Indian research institutions under the Human Space Flight Centre's (HSFC) coordination — studying the cognitive effects of screen use, microbial adaptation, muscle atrophy, and crop resilience in microgravity.

qa.question: Why was Axiom Mission-4 considered strategically important for India's Gaganyaan programme?
qa.answer: It served as a test case/preparatory exercise to assess India's readiness for Gaganyaan, India's own indigenous human spaceflight programme — giving ISRO real experience with astronaut training, experiment design, and mission operations ahead of Gaganyaan's own crewed flight.
